title: thalictrum simplex subsp. galioides
intro: Thalictrum simplex subsp. galioides, commonly known as Meadow Rue, is a perennial plant belonging to the Buttercup family, characterized by its delicate white or pale purple flowers and fern-like foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, making it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Meadow Rue include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve respiratory function, making it a valuable remedy for those suffering from bronchitis, asthma, and other respiratory conditions. Meadow Rue can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in herbal medicine to soothe respiratory issues and promote overall well-being.
